title: DK016
description: current — a dub techno track for L64. 116 BPM, C minor. Cm9 → Fm7 → Gm7 → Abmaj7 chord stab progression rotating every 8 bars; each stab routed wet into a 3/16-note delay loop with 0.68 feedback and a 2.2kHz lowpass inside the feedback loop (the Basic Channel signature — each repeat darkens). Sparse 909 kick on quarters, sub on C1 with slow LFO breathe, hihat shuffle, vinyl crackle bed. Drag rides the master LPF; tap drops a manual extra stab into the dub delay loop. Visualizer is L64's per-pixel curl-noise streamline shader. Wheelhouse: Maurizio's Phylyps Trak II, Yagya's Rhythm of Snow. No jambot, no samples.

title: DK015
description: slice — Octatrack-style glitch sampler over the weather field. 124 BPM. Four source buffers (Am9 chord pad, vowel-formant noise sweep, percussion hit, FM metallic shimmer) rendered offline at startup, then chopped and triggered with per-step parameter locks: random slice start, variable playback rate, occasional reverse, stutter retriggers (4–6 within one step). Kicks bypass the bitcrush bus to stay punchy; everything else through ~6-bit waveshaper + tilt LP at 5.5kHz, with a parallel send to a dotted-eighth feedback delay (dub-techno influence). Visualizer = L60 weather field with one bloom per slice (color per buffer family). No jambot, no samples on disk.

title: DK014
description: chamber — dub for the aurora. 122 BPM. Soft 4/4 kick + sub anchor, hi-hat shuffle, Am7 → Dm9 → Em7 chord stabs through a 369ms (3/16 note) feedback delay with a darkening lowpass in the loop. Vinyl crackle bed, slow master-LPF sweep across each 32-bar pass. Visualizer is the L59 aurora — chord stabs become lime substorms locked to the beat. No jambot, no samples.

title: DK013
description: techno on a caustic floor. 132 BPM. 32-bar arrangement: intro / build / bridge / peak. Knob-jammed hat decays. Visualizer locked to the kick. No jambot — synthesized straight in WebAudio.

title: DK012
description: Clinical minimal. 128 BPM. Bone dry. Concrete.

title: DK011
description: Dub techno. E minor. 118 BPM. Less is more.

title: DK010
description: Jeff Mills 909 session. Tension through subtraction. 132 BPM. JT90.

title: DK009
description: Dub techno. Root and fifth through fog. 118 BPM. D minor.

title: DK008
description: Dub techno. The delay chain is the composition. 118 BPM. D minor.

title: DK007

title: DK006
description: Plastikman-style minimal. The filter is the composition. Ghost bars. The 1% rule. 126 BPM. C minor.

title: DK005
description: JT10 arpeggio dissolves into reverb. 80 bars. Rise, peak, dissolve, drift, return with kick. 118 BPM. A minor.

title: DK002
description: 52 bars of tribal machinery. Six acts. Decay curves that breathe.
